message:
feat(search/filter_compiler): accept `agent` as alias for `sender` in SearchFilter; add filter_by_agent_alias unit test

Users and MCP clients have been sending `agent=<name>` on search
filter payloads expecting it to scope by sender, but the filter
compiler only looked at `filter.sender` and silently ignored
`filter.agent`. Fix that at the compile site so both spellings
produce the same Tantivy `Term` query against the sender STRING
field.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/filter_compiler.rs
- `compile_filters` now reads the sender via
  `filter.sender.as_ref().or(filter.agent.as_ref())`, i.e. prefer
  the explicit `sender` field when set, otherwise fall back to
  `agent`. Comment updated to make the alias relationship obvious
  to the next reader: "Sender filter (exact match on STRING field).
  `agent` is an alias."
- New unit test `filter_by_agent_alias`: builds an index with the
  existing `setup_index()` helper, issues a filter with only
  `agent: Some("BlueLake".to_string())` set, runs it through
  `search_with_filter`, and asserts the same single-doc result set
  (`vec![1]`) the existing `filter_by_sender` test returns. Locks
  in the alias behaviour against future filter-compiler rewrites.
